title: Type mismatch resolving associated type
explanation: This error occurs when an associated type in a trait implementation does not match the expected type. This commonly happens with Iterator::Item, Future::Output, or custom trait associated types.
error_message: error[E0271]: type mismatch resolving `<std::vec::IntoIter<&str> as Iterator>::Item == i32`

fixes.description: Pass an iterator with the correct Item type
fixes.code:
let v = vec![1, 2, 3];
sum_iter(v.into_iter()); // Vec<i32> gives Item = i32

fixes.description: Transform the iterator to the expected type
Change the function to accept the correct type

tip: Associated types are like generic parameters fixed by the implementation. Iterator<Item = i32> means the iterator yields i32 values. Check what Item type your iterator produces.
